title: Astronomers spot blue straggler star hosting brown dwarf companion
content: This breakthrough by the team from the Indian Institute of Astrophysics, Aryabhatta Research Institute of Observational Sciences, Gauhati University, and INAF-Catania Astrophysical Observatory, Italy, could reshape astronomers’ understanding of how stars evolve

title: Government polytechnics in Tamil Nadu record near total placement this year
content: According to officials, a reason for this could be the very nature of the diploma courses, which equip students with more hands-on, vocational, and trade-specific skills

title: P&T apartment residents to lay indefinite siege to GCDA office
content: Residents have been protesting the persistent leakage since 78 families moved into the apartments built by GCDA in January 2024

title: Horse trading at horse speed: DMK chief Stalin on AIADMK MLAs joining TVK
content: Three MLAs who were part of the S.P. Velumani faction of the AIADMK met Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar and submitted their resignation letters, before joining the TVK

title: DSEL issues order to increase egg distribution coverage in Karnataka schools to 90%
content: The DSEL has instructed officials to create awareness among students about the benefits of egg consumption, and has ordered that the target of 80% egg distribution should be achieved by September 2026.

title: Intense speculation as Siddaramaiah and Shivakumar head to Delhi for meeting on May 26
content: Though Congress General Secretary in-charge of Karnataka Randeep Singh Surjewala said it would be a consultation meeting ahead of the elections to Rajya Sabha and Legislative Council, speculation on the leadership issue has refused to die down

title: Thoothukudi schoolgirl sexual assault and murder case: Man gets death penalty on two counts
content: The girl had gone missing in a scrub forest area around 250 metres from her residence on the evening of March 10; she was found murdered the next day

title: Madhu lynching case: Kerala HC enhances sentence of 12 accused to life imprisonment, acquits one
content: Madhu’s mother and the Kerala government had filed appeals seeking enhanced punishment for the accused. The mentally challenged tribal youth was killed following the allegation that he had stolen rice from a grocery shop in February 2018

title: Oil PSUs report sharp rise in demand for diesel, petrol in Telangana
content: Diesel sales up by 12.6%, petrol by 9% in first three weeks of May amid intensified agricultural, harvesting activities, retail consumers of other suppliers increasingly shifting towards PSUs outlet due to competitive pricing

title: Twisha Sharma death: SC pained by handling of case, asks media to exercise restraint; CBI team dispatched to Bhopal
content: Better to have a divorced daughter than face such an unfortunate incident, says Solicitor General during SC hearing

title: Farmers are an integral part of alco-bev industry, do not leave them behind: Timmapur urges industry
content: Most raw materials required for breweries — including barley, grains, fruits, and other agricultural produce — are cultivated by farmers. The government is committed to grow the industry beyond Bengaluru

title: Over 300 tourists stranded in Gulmarg’s high-altitude cable cars rescued
content: Panic gripped the tourists stranded in 65 gondolas after a technical slag, even as the weather turned inclement, with strong winds and rains lashing Gulmarg; the rescue operation lasted over four hours; 15 SDRF teams were deployed

title: ‘Rape, murder’ of minor girl sparks outrage in Kashmir, one arrested
content: The victim, who is believed to be around 12, went missing on Saturday and her body was recovered on Sunday

title: NIA conducts searches of banned Jamaat-e-Islami’s trusts, associations in Srinagar, Shopian
content: During its investigations, the NIA said, it had emerged that the JeI had been actively involved in collecting terror funds, and fuelling terrorism in the Kashmir Valley and other parts of India

title: Inclusion of Maithili in CBSE curriculum will connect future generation to their roots: Bihar CM
content: BJP leader and MP from Darbhanga Parliamentary seat, Gopal Ji Thakur, met Union Education Minister and Minister of State for Education, and demanded the inclusion of Maithili language as a subject in the CBSE curriculum.

title: Naidu calls for blue economy vision plan for Andhra Pradesh coastline
content: Chief Minister directs officials to prepare a comprehensive strategy focusing on sustainable coastal development, marine resource utilisation, aquaculture value addition and ecological protection

title: Three AIADMK MLAs resign from posts in Tamil Nadu, join TVK
content: Assembly Speaker J.C.D. Prabhakar accepts their resignations. AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami and AMMK founder T.T.V. Dhinakaran accuse TVK of horse trading.

title: Glitches in CBSE portal leave Class 12 students seeking re-evaluation hassled
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has sought a detailed report from CBSE, deputed technical team from IIT Madras and IIT Kanpur to assist the board in solving tech issues

title: Amit Shah to visit border areas over next three weeks
content: During his visits to border outposts along Pakistan and Bangladesh borders, Union Home Minister is expected to take stock of security situation with State government and Border Security Force officials.

title: UDF government will scrutinise steps taken by previous LDF government in MSC Elsa 3 disaster, says V.D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister says government closely monitoring fuel price hike and its possible impact on common man

title: Health Minister reviews Ebola preparedness and surveillance measures
content: On May 24th India issued a travel advisory, telling citizens to avoid non-essential travel to the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda and South Sudan

title: Opposition parties criticise CM  Vijay over a spate of crimes across Tamil Nadu
content: “Is this Tamil Nadu or Uttar Pradesh? Where is your Singapen  Athiradi Padai ? Why is the Chief Minister, who delivered valour-tinged dialogues before the election, refusing to open his mouth,” asks Leader of the Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin

title: The Hindu Group wins top honours at Kyoorius, ABBY Awards
content: The campaign, ‘Written by Journalists’, has won the prestigious Black Elephant at Kyoorius Marketing Awards, recognising it as the finest work of the year

title: President Droupadi Murmu confers 66 Padma awards
content: President Droupadi Murmu confers the Padma Vibhushan on legendary Bollywood actor Dharmendra posthumously and violin virtuoso N. Rajam for exceptional contribution to their respective fields

title: Mid-air scare: 300 tourists stranded after technical snag hits cable car in Gulmarg
content: Hundreds of tourists faced an unexpected halt at Gulmarg's famous Gondola cable car service due to a technical glitch on Monday.  Officials confirmed that both phases of the popular attraction were suspended.  Trained rescue teams from SDRF and NDRF safely evacuated all stranded visitors from the cabins. Restoration efforts are currently in progress.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Did Rubio call Trump stupid? US State Dept scrubs remark, sparks debate
content: The State Department deleted a social media post featuring Secretary of State Mike Pompeo's remarks after critics suggested he called President Trump "stupid." This incident highlights the sensitivity around the word "stupid" in the Trump era, a term the President frequently uses to describe opponents and even allies.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system'
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Google Search can now do a lot of things, but ‘searching’ isn't really one of them
content: Google's Search isn't a search engine anymore. At I/O 2026, the company officially rebuilt its 25-year-old product around Gemini 3.5 Flash—a box that now accepts photos, files, and video, generates custom interactive pages on the fly, and deploys AI agents to monitor the web indefinitely on your behalf. With AI Overviews at 2.5 billion monthly users and AI Mode crossing one billion, Google isn't pitching a future. It's documenting a shift that already happened.

title: US Immigration authority issues clarification for H-1B visa holders
content: USCIS has clarified a new Green Card policy. This policy could have required many to apply from abroad. However, USCIS now states that applicants with national interest or economic benefit may continue their current process. This offers relief to many H-1B visa holders and tech industry workers. The agency is reasserting its interpretation of immigration law.

title: UP woman electrocutes husband before forcing poison into his body; 4, including lover, held
content: A wife allegedly conspired with her lover, sister, and sister's boyfriend to murder her husband after he objected to her extramarital affair. The four accused, aged 30-35, have been arrested after the postmortem revealed electric shock injuries and poison, confirming the murder plot.

title: Not Phil Salt! Former cricketer says Venkatesh Iyer should open with Kohli
content: Qualifier 1 between Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans will decide the first finalist of IPL 2026.

title: Stock market holiday: Will NSE and BSE remain closed for Bakri Eid this week?
content: Stock markets will observe a shortened trading week with a closure on Thursday, May 28, for Bakri Eid. This marks the second holiday this month. Investors can anticipate several more market holidays throughout 2026, including those for Muharram, Ganesh Chaturthi, and Diwali. While some holidays fall on weekends, a special Muhurat Trading session is expected.

title: Amanda Nunes and Nina Nunes’ combined net worth in 2026 explored
content: MMA power couple Amanda and Nina Nunes boast an estimated combined net worth of $5-7 million by 2026. Amanda's historic dual-division championship reign and lucrative UFC career, coupled with Nina's steady run and coaching, fueled their financial success.  Their investments and private lifestyle underscore a remarkable journey from combat sports to respected family life.

title: Taylor Swift and Blake Lively feud takes new turn before Kelce wedding
content: Taylor Swift and popular Hollywood actress Blake Lively, have remained distant from one another for more than a year now after the pop star was dragged into the lawsuit with Justin Baldoni. As guests are being invited just weeks ahead of Taylor Swift’s lavish wedding to the Kansas City Chiefs’ star player, Travis Kelce, it seems the billionaire pop star has made her feelings clear about Blake Lively.

title: Maple Leafs stars send strong offseason message to John Chayka plans
content: Major roster issues are reportedly on the radar of Toronto Maple Leafs management as the NHL offseason gets underway with Auston Matthews and William Nylander. The franchise’s stars believe the team needs help at second-line center, wing depth and right-shot defenseman positions. Toronto is trying to rebuild a struggling roster after a disappointing 2025-26 NHL season, and their comments put pressure on John Chayka.

title: Rupee rally: Currency rises 34 paise against dollar amid hopes of US-Iran peace deal
content: The Indian rupee strengthened significantly against the US dollar, closing at 95.26, driven by positive global sentiment surrounding a potential US-Iran peace deal and easing West Asian tensions. RBI Governor's assurance of orderly forex market movements and positive remarks on the rupee's valuation also boosted confidence.

title: Virat Kohli to Sooryavanshi: How top batters rank in IPL 'false shot' charts
content: Despite batting at a high strike rate, Rahul has the lowest false shot percentage this season at 12.1

title: From Caracas to Havana: Why Trump's Venezuela playbook may fail in communist Cuba
content: US president Donald Trump's presidency has shifted to muscular interventionism, beginning with the swift overthrow of Venezuela's Maduro. Now, the focus turns to Cuba, but unlike Venezuela, the island presents a more cohesive political structure, a disciplined military, and a population prepared for conflict, making the 'Venezuela model' unlikely to succeed.

title: ‘Willing to listen to people’: Govt open to feedback on LTCG, STCG taxes, says Sitharaman
content: Finance minister Nirmala Sitharaman expressed openness to investor concerns regarding capital gains tax, assuring that the government will consider stakeholder suggestions. She also defended recent fuel price hikes, attributing them to global crude oil costs and highlighting the government's prior absorption of excise duties.

title: ‘I wish I could hand out world peace and...’: Quote of the day by Christopher Walken
content: Christopher Walken's unforgettable remark from Batman Returns, 'I wish I could hand out world peace and unconditional love, wrapped in a big bow,' carries a poignant message that transcends the film's villainy. It reflects a heartfelt aspiration for universal peace and compassion, echoing the sentiments of countless hearts around the globe.

title: Fuel shock: How price hike changes what you eat, what you buy and how you travel
content: Fuel prices have risen sharply, adding to household expenses and transportation costs. This surge, driven by global events, is straining supply chains and impacting everyday goods. Oil marketing companies are seeing stock gains. The government faces a balancing act between consumer relief and revenue. The economic outlook remains a focus amidst these challenges.

title: Govt says fuel stocks adequate amid panic buying in some states
content: Government assures sufficient fuel stocks amid panic buying and dryouts in select states. Joint Secretary Sujata Sharma cited increased demand and logistical issues, not shortages.  Fuel prices have risen significantly, with state-run companies still facing losses. Citizens are urged to conserve fuel and explore alternatives.

title: Bengaluru sees 1 economic offence every 2.5 hours
content: Bengaluru faces a surge in economic crimes. Over three years, one offense occurred every 2.5 hours. Forgery, cheating, and fraud dominate these cases. This indicates a shift from physical theft to financial deception. Police face challenges due to cross-state networks and digital methods. The city's crime profile is changing.

title: ‘Gave 40 tolas gold, Rs 1 crore plot’: UK-returned techie alleges dowry harassment, assault
content: Medipally police have filed a case against an Uppal resident and his family for dowry harassment and assault. A London-returned IT professional accused them of demanding additional dowry, threatening her, and assaulting her, leading to her hospitalization. This follows an earlier domestic violence case registered against the accused.

title: 'Not in my control': Patidar on IPL final moving from Bengaluru to Ahmedabad
content: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will face Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1 for a direct spot in the IPL 2026 final, which will be held in Ahmedabad. RCB captain Rajat Patidar acknowledged the challenging conditions for bowlers this season, with even 200-plus scores not guaranteeing safety. He emphasized the team's focus on execution and staying calm under pressure.

title: 'Doubted child’s paternity': Man kills father, wife over suspected affair
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Bulandshahr where a 28-year-old man allegedly murdered his father and wife, driven by suspicions of an illicit affair. The man reportedly used his father's licensed pistol after a heated argument. Police have arrested the accused, who claimed to have found incriminating evidence of the alleged relationship.

title: Tyreek Hill turns to shocking new career move amid NFL uncertainty
content: Free agent wide receiver Tyreek Hill has embraced DJing in Miami while recovering from knee surgery. He finds enjoyment in EDM music. Despite being linked to several teams for several months since his release from the Miami Dolphins, no NFL franchise has signed him as of yet. Hill’s new hobby could potentially enhance his football skills. The incident mirrors the positive impact seen with Bijan Robinson’s DJing experience in 2024.

title: The heat that hurts, the rain that heals: Inside India’s monsoon machine
content: Delhi's intense summer heat, pushing past 45°C, is not just a discomfort but a vital part of a natural engine. This brutal heat drives the monsoon, aids soil health, ripens fruits, and suppresses pests, playing a crucial role in India's agricultural and ecological cycles.

title: Poor home record to toothless bowling: Inside Delhi Capitals’ failed IPL campaign
content: DC ended sixth in the table with seven wins and seven defeats, extending their playoff drought to five straight seasons. It was also the second consecutive season in which the combination of head coach Hemang Badani and captain Axar Patel failed to guide the franchise into the top four.

title: Pilot aborts takeoff after technical snag in UP deputy CM Brajesh Pathak’s plane
content: Uttar Pradesh Deputy CM Brajesh Pathak's flight from Moradabad was aborted due to a technical snag in the UP government aircraft. The pilot detected an issue while rolling on the runway, leading to a precautionary takeoff abort. Pathak later observed smoke from the aircraft, and an inquiry is expected.

title: Ranveer Singh breaks silence after film workers’ body issues directive over ‘Don 3’ exit
content: FWICE issued a non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh over his exit from 'Don 3', after he failed to respond to three formal notices. Ranveer's spokesperson said he chose to maintain silence with dignity. FWICE President BN Tiwari declared that no one, not even a superstar, is above industry rules.

title: 'Detect, delete and deport': Bengal govt opens first illegal migrant 'holding centre'
content: West Bengal's BJP government has launched its 'detect, delete and deport' policy, opening a holding center in Malda for illegal foreign nationals. Nine suspected Bangladeshi nationals are currently housed there, awaiting verification and legal procedures. This initiative aims to streamline the deportation process for apprehended individuals, as mandated by a new national law.

title: ‘If he knew history ...’: Iran mocks Marco Rubio's Taj Mahal visit
content: Iran's Hyderabad Consulate criticized US Secretary of State Marco Rubio's Taj Mahal visit, asserting Iranian architects built the monument while the US threatens Iranian civilization. This follows Rubio's India trip and comes after Iran rejected his energy market claims. The consulate's remarks also alluded to past US threats during regional conflicts.

title: Army makes its artillery arm more lethal by giving its guns automatic aiming capability
content: The Indian Army is enhancing its 105mm field guns with automatic aiming technology, a significant modernization step. This computer-driven system drastically reduces manual effort, improving accuracy, response times, and survivability for artillery crews. This upgrade aligns with the broader Field Artillery Rationalisation Plan, aiming for a more potent and efficient artillery force.

title: Kerala: 24-year-old woman dies after consuming acid over 'dowry' harassment; husband in custody
content: A 24-year-old woman tragically died after allegedly consuming acid at her husband's home in Kasaragod. Fathimath Sufaida's family claims she faced harassment over dowry. An altercation preceded the incident, with her husband reportedly preventing her departure and destroying her phone. Police have taken the husband into custody.

title: Buying a home in NCR: How much it costs in Delhi, Gurugram and Noida
content: The National Capital Region is witnessing a dramatic spike in property prices. With the impending Jewar airport, Noida's real estate landscape is evolving into a high-end hub, where luxury properties are fetching premium rates. Meanwhile, Gurugram's realty scene is booming as revised circle rates elevate property values, particularly in emerging neighbourhoods. Meanwhile, Delhi is about to revise its circle rates for the first time since 2014.

title: Cockroach Janta Party founder moves Delhi HC after X account blocked
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke has approached the Delhi High Court challenging the Centre's blocking of the group's social media accounts. The satirical movement, which gained viral traction discussing unemployment and exam leaks, saw its X account suspended on national security grounds. Dipke also alleged hacking of other accounts.

title: Russia's Lavrov Urges US Diplomat Evacuation From Kyiv In Call With Rubio
content: According to the Russia's foreign ministry, Sergei Lavrov held a telephone conversation with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on May 25.

title: Congress Lost Dalit Support For Delayed Action: Rahul Gandhi Ahead Of UP Polls
content: Sources present at the meeting revealed that Rahul Gandhi praised Bahujan Samaj Pary founder Kanshi Ram, noting that he succeeded in uniting the Dalit community and instilling a sense of self-confidence in them.

title: Vijay Announces Farm Loan Waivers, Extended Screening Of Movies
content: The waivers were announced after a high-level review meeting on the matter which was attended by Chief Minister Vijay.

title: 300 Tourists Rescued After Gulmarg Gondola Technical Issue Sparks Panic
content: A massive multi-agency rescue operation involving the Army, Jammu and Kashmir Police, paramilitary forces and Civil Defence teams was launched immediately to evacuate tourists trapped inside multiple chair cars.

title: Big Change In Karnataka Soon? Siddaramaiah, DK Shivakumar Land In Delhi
content: The subject of discussion for the Delhi meeting, the 77-year-old Chief Minister said, is not known to him.

title: A Death Threat In Court, Then 2 Murders Within 2 Hours In Bihar
content: Tensions had been simmering between the two families for the past six months over issues involving harassment, physical altercations, and land disputes.

title: CBI Takes Over Twisha Sharma Death Probe
content: The team of the Special Crime unit met the Bhopal police officials and collected the necessary documents and evidence.

title: Aviation Body Issues Ebola Precaution Guidelines For Uganda, Congo Airlines
content: Airlines must ensure compliance with health screening requirements, including identification of suspected cases, immediate isolation measures and coordination with airport health authorities.

title: What Iran, US And Israel Stand To Gain And Lose If Deal Takes Shape
content: Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he and Trump had agreed that "any final agreement with Iran must eliminate the nuclear threat entirely".

title: Hormuz May Reopen 30 Days After Iran, US Strike Peace Deal: Report
content: The U.S. and Iran are discussing a plan to open the Strait of Hormuz about 30 days after the two countries reach a deal to end hostilities, the Nikkei newspaper reported on Monday citing a Middle East diplomatic source.

title: Madhya Pradesh's Dhar To Get Maa Saraswati Lok, Raja Bhoj Research Institute
content: This comes days after the Madhya Pradesh High Court declared the disputed Bhojshala complex a temple dedicated to Goddess Saraswati.

title: US Iran War Live Updates: Hormuz May Reopen 30 Days After Iran, US Strike Peace Deal
content: US Iran War Live Updates: Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf said Iran's armed forces had rebuilt their capabilities during the ceasefire.

title: Trump Spotlights Asim Munir, Leaves Pak PM Out Of Abraham Accords Vision
content: In a lengthy statement posted on Truth Social, Trump said negotiations with Iran were "proceeding nicely" and framed the moment as a potential turning point.

title: At 32.4 Degrees Celsius, Delhi Records Warmest May Night In 14 years
content: The maximum temperature at Safdarjung, the city's base station, settled at 43.5 degrees Celsius, 3.3 degrees above normal.

title: Explained: How NEET Probe Is Unravelling Maharashtra's Coaching Economy
content: What began as allegations of irregularities in the NEET-UG examination has now expanded into a widening investigation involving coaching operators, school administrators, alleged middlemen, translators linked to the National Testing Agency.

title: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: CBI Registers Case, To Begin Investigation On Tuesday
content: Twisha Sharma Death Case Reaches Supreme Court Hearing LIVE Updates: A Supreme Court bench headed by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Justice Vipul M Pancholi will hear the case.

title: 'I-PAC Had No Manners, Spoke Rudely': Trinamool MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar
content: Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar attributed Trinamool's loss of five of the seven Assembly seats in the limits of Barasat Parliamentary constituency to the "cumulative effect" of anti-incumbency and Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls.

title: NDTV Nava Punjab Summit Highlights: "AAP A National Party": Bhagwant Mann On 'Arvind Kejriwal Super CM' Charges
content: NDTV Nava Punjab Summit Live: Punjab Chief Minister Bhagwant Singh Mann, Chief Secretary KAP Sinha and DGP Gaurav Yadav are among the key figures speaking at the NDTV Nava Punjab Summit being held in Chandigarh today.

title: Trump Adds Fresh Uncertainty To Iran Peace Talks With 'Abraham Accords' Post
content: This comes on top of an already long list of requirements that includes keeping the Strait of Hormuz open and Iran abandoning uranium enrichment entirely.

title: The Abraham Accord plus: Trump aims for grand bargain tying Islamic world with Israel
content: the sudden and sweeping proposal, unveiled in a lengthy Truth Social post, stunned diplomats across the Middle East and South Asia . the proposal triggered resistance from Pakistan and unease in Riyadh and doha . "it should be mandatory that all these countries simultaneously, at a minimum, simultaneously, sign onto the Abraham Accords," Trump said .

title: DGCA issues Ebola preparedness SOP for airlines operating flights linked to Uganda, Congo
content: DGCA issued detailed Ebola preparedness protocols for airlines . it mandates health screening measures, onboard isolation procedures and compulsory self-declaration forms for passengers arriving in India . the order, issued following a world health organisation alert on the outbreak, directs airlines to identify symptomatic passengers onboard .

title: CPM condemns ban on Cockroach Janta Party, accuses BJP of intolerance
content: the Communist Party of India condemned the ban on social media handles of the Cockroach Janta Party and the attack on its creator abhijeet Dipke . the satirical political movement founded on may 16 emerged in response to remarks made by chief justice of india .

title: CPM accuses Cong 'attitude towards opposition' as factor hindering oppn unity against RSS-BJP
content: the Communist Party of India (Marxist) cautioned that strengthening opposition unity was a necessity to counter the “growth of right wing forces” . he said that the growth of right-wing forces necessitates the unity of all Left, progressive, secular, and democratic forces .

title: Tarique Rahman to visit China in his 1st overseas visit as Bangladesh PM
content: the visit would mark his first overseas visit since the formation of the new govt . a pending invitation from PM Narendra Modi and Bhutan initially being chosen as the destination of his first foreign tour . the engagement appears to be driven in part by renewed efforts to secure Chinese financing for the long-delayed Teesta river restoration project .

title: Iran throws architectural shade on Marco Rubio's hot Taj Mahal visit
content: the latest chapter in the snark diplomacy began innocently enough when the US secretary of state and his wife visited the Taj Mahal in Agra on Sunday . he dutifully posed on the famous marble bench – hot enough to turn his batootie into bacon, someone noted . the only one I ever knew about was the one in New Jersey that the President used to have .

title: Govt wrote thrice to Delhi Gymkhana since Sept last year to clear Rs 48-crore rent
content: the land and development office had written three letters to the club management since sept last year to clear around Rs 48 crore in rent that DGC owed to it . some club members said they were not intimated about the pending due earlier . there was response to queries sent to two govt-appointed GC members on why the due was not paid .
